expectations.text: Verdict is KILL, stated in the first ~5 lines (early-warning, not buried under preamble)
expectations.evidence: Line 1: '# Verdict: kill it. This session is a confirmed doom loop.'

expectations.text: Failure class is identified as behavioural-doom-loop (not dead-deal)
expectations.evidence: Labels as 'doom loop' but the substantive analysis describes a dead deal: 'provably stuck', 'no sequence of legal moves from this state that can flip a single new card', 'position itself is dead'. This contradicts the user's existing classification of seed 3689552861 in DATASET_NOTES.md ('Cause is bad AI decisions, not a bad deck'). Conflates behavioural with structural.

expectations.text: Cites the 4D/5C oscillation between cols 3 and 4 with numeric counts (not just "there's a loop")
expectations.evidence: Mentions 'three legal moves' and 'two no-op shuffles' but never identifies the specific 4D / 5C between cols 3-4 oscillation, nor counts it. Only the recentMoves field is parsed, not the session-wide sweep.

expectations.text: References same-seed baseline 645d03 (this export is the comparison arm)
expectations.evidence: No mention of 645d03 anywhere. The baseline-vs-comparison-arm framing is missed entirely — without that context this gets read as a fresh dead-deal session.

expectations.text: Does NOT include a dedicated provider-error-rate concern section
expectations.evidence: Line 23 mentions the 57% error rate but explicitly notes 'isn't the story here' — does not dedicate a separate concern section. Borderline but acceptable.

expectations.text: Reports plateau length and the (foundationCards, faceDownTotal) tuple
